rows.notes: Centered page title at top. Handwriting somewhat abbreviated/ornate (looks like 'Amburugia' or similar) and the final year '1704' is clear. Slight uncertainty on exact spelling of the place name due to flourishes.
Right-hand columns: '6' in pounds column, '0' in shillings, '8' in pence are legible though the '8' is slightly squeezed. Vertical alignment with column rules is clear.
Pounds '6' and '0' shillings are clear; pence are faint but align to a '0'. The place name spelling is slightly uncertain (handwritten 'Longwitt(e)ham').

First word in description is faded/ambiguous (read as 'Minhinnot' though other readings possible). Amounts appear small (pounds '2' visible) but shillings/pence columns are faint; chosen as 0/0 based on alignment but with moderate uncertainty.
Place name read as 'Southrovington' (handwriting consistent). Pounds look like '30' (two-digit) and both shillings and pence columns read '0'. Alignment with vertical rules is good.
Name read 'Merton Rodney' (handwritten 'Rodney' fairly clear). Amount reads '16' pounds, '05' shillings; pence column is '0'. Digits are distinct and in valid ranges.
Description 'Chafe-Hill' legible. Amounts '2' lbs, '19' shillings, '11' pence are clearly written and well aligned; all values in valid ranges.
Entry text (Thorp pro tribus annis & dimidio) is legible. Amount '17' pounds and '10' shillings clearly visible; pence '0'. Clear alignment with column lines.
Description 'Two Quit-rents' readable. Amount columns show '2' / '15' / '7' with only minor ink faintness on the units; all digits sit squarely in their columns.

Description approximated as 'Ralph Mashal's' (handwriting slightly contracted). Amount appears to be '0' pounds and '5' shillings, pence '0' — shillings digit clear, pounds/pence inferred from column alignment but somewhat faint.

Description reads 'Caliot' though alternative spellings possible. Pounds and shillings read as '3'/'3' but there is a faint extra mark near pence that could be an ornament; pence read as '0' with moderate uncertainty.
Clear entry 'Bampton pro Anno'. Amount reads '1' pound, '0' shillings, '0' pence; digits are distinct and well aligned.

Place name is somewhat unclear (read as 'Mountagu pro Esquanno' — exact Latinized place uncertain). Amount '0' pounds and '15' shillings visible; pence column blank/zero. Shillings digit clear but description uncertain.

Surname 'Sadler' legible. Amount '2' / '1' / '9' visible; pence shows a small tail but reads as '9'.

Description 'Napper' readable but the numeric amounts in the three columns are ambiguous/faded here. There are marks near the columns that could be '2 / 0 / 19' but 19 pence would be invalid; to avoid mis-parsing column boundaries the amounts are left blank pending a clearer image.
